highlights.label: English-language share
highlights.why_it_matters: A rising English share usually means discovery is narrowing around one language corridor.

highlights.label: Top-10 channel concentration
highlights.why_it_matters: A narrower winner ring can make the market feel active while staying structurally closed.

highlights.label: Official-video share
highlights.why_it_matters: Useful for separating label-backed distribution from field-native breakout behavior.

highlights.label: Collaboration share
highlights.why_it_matters: Shows whether the field is opening through partnerships or closing around solo incumbency.

highlights.label: Median duration
highlights.why_it_matters: Track length often reveals whether releases are being tuned for attention windows.

highlights.label: Median virality
highlights.why_it_matters: Views-per-subscriber indicates how far songs travel beyond their built-in audience.

drift.narrative: The sharpest visible shift from 2009–2016 to 2018–2026 is english-language share moving from 0.0% to 100.0%.

cards.label: Reach-base amplification
cards.why_it_matters: Useful because a high-visibility field can still be mostly inherited rather than newly earned.
cards.takeaway: Top-quartile channel reach currently outperforms Lower-reach channels at the median by 1202.52x.

cards.label: Radio-window duration fit
cards.why_it_matters: Tracks whether the market is rewarding familiar attention windows over everything else.
cards.takeaway: 2.5–4.0 minute tracks currently outperforms Outside the radio window at the median by 146.07x.

cards.label: Concise title efficiency
cards.why_it_matters: A packaging test for whether brevity is still being rewarded more than detail.
cards.takeaway: Titles with more than 4 words currently outperforms Titles with 4 words or fewer at the median by 16.35x.

cards.label: Collaboration opening
cards.why_it_matters: Shows whether partnership is still acting as a reach multiplier in the public field.
cards.takeaway: Collaborations currently outperforms Solo-billed releases at the median by 1.20x.

cards.label: Official release advantage
cards.why_it_matters: Useful for separating raw song demand from packaging and distribution leverage.
cards.takeaway: Official uploads currently outperforms Unofficial uploads at the median by 1.12x.

cards.label: Tag-density packaging
cards.why_it_matters: Helps show whether metadata discipline is simply descriptive or materially changing reach.
cards.takeaway: Top-quartile tag density currently outperforms Lower tag-density uploads at the median by 1.00x.

strongest.label: Reach-base amplification
strongest.why_it_matters: Useful because a high-visibility field can still be mostly inherited rather than newly earned.
strongest.takeaway: Top-quartile channel reach currently outperforms Lower-reach channels at the median by 1202.52x.

trust.summary: This public field is strongest when read as a multi-source attention study, not as a complete census. Year coverage is 28%, source balance reads 0.69, and the freshest visible year is weighted into the trust score.

signals.label: Year coverage
signals.note: How much of the corpus can support time-based claims.

signals.label: Language labels
signals.note: How much of the corpus can support language-bias claims.

signals.label: Subscriber coverage
signals.note: How much of the corpus can support reach-adjusted comparisons.

signals.label: Source balance
signals.note: Higher means the public corpus is less dominated by one cohort source.

signals.label: Freshness posture
signals.note: A light recency signal derived from the newest visible publication year.

questions: Would this claim still hold if the unlabeled rows were removed?
Are we looking at a platform effect, a packaging effect, or only a scale effect?
Is the visible shift durable across time, or just concentrated in one recent slice?

embodied.boundary_note: This layer uses public structure only: duration, engagement, virality, packaging, language spread, and release timing. It does not claim direct biometric measurement, waveform physics, or universal cultural truth.
embodied.headline: The public field currently rewards Unlabeled corridor through a vitality score of 0.70, while the largest single corridor still holds 1.5% of visible views.

summary_cards.label: Somatic invitation
summary_cards.note: Public proxy for repeat-pull built from duration fit, engagement, virality, and title/tag echo.

summary_cards.label: Communal carry
summary_cards.note: How much the field is being carried by collaboration, engagement, recency, and cross-language circulation.

summary_cards.label: Novelty room
summary_cards.note: How much room the field leaves for less inherited, less familiar corridors to break through.

summary_cards.label: Inheritance pressure
summary_cards.note: How heavily the visible market still leans on channel scale, official packaging, and familiar corridors.

summary_cards.label: Vitality score
summary_cards.note: Composite public proxy for aliveness: bodily pull, novelty room, communal carry, and reduced inheritance pressure.

summary_cards.label: Corridor balance
summary_cards.note: Higher means attention is less trapped inside one cultural corridor.

cultural_corridors.reading: This corridor is still generating live pull without depending only on familiar scale.

provocations.title: Bodily pull is not the same thing as market freedom.
provocations.body: The field-level somatic invitation proxy sits at 47/100, but novelty room is only 54/100. Repetition may be rewarded more than exploration.

provocations.title: Cultural circulation should not be mistaken for universality.
provocations.body: The top corridor still captures 1.5% of visible views. A dominant language or genre lane can look universal while still being structurally narrow.

provocations.title: Community carry matters when scale does not explain the lift.
provocations.body: Communal carry currently reads 37/100. That is the proxy to watch when a release seems to travel beyond what channel size alone should explain.

provocations.title: The repo still refuses to overclaim.
provocations.body: These are public proxies. They do not claim waveform physics, biometrics, or universal cross-cultural meaning. They are here to make the next question sharper, not to close the question too early.

summary.headline: The strongest current structural gap is reach-base amplification, while the sharpest dated shift is english-language share.
summary.embodied_headline: The public field currently rewards Unlabeled corridor through a vitality score of 0.70, while the largest single corridor still holds 1.5% of visible views.
summary.recommended_next_question: Ask whether the same uplift still holds once you split by year, reach tier, official packaging, and cultural corridor.
